text:
<think>I should begin by clarifying that the melody for "Twinkle, Twinkle, Little Star" was not originally composed for this song, but pre-dates it.</think>
The tune most commonly associated with "Twinkle, Twinkle, Little Star" is a French melody called "Ah! vous dirai-je, Maman."
<think>Now, I should specify when and how this melody originated to provide more context.</think>
This melody first appeared in France in the 18th century and was published in 1761.
<think>I should address the question of authorship, since the user asked who composed it.</think>
The composer of the original melody is unknown, as it was a popular folk tune before it was widely published.
<think>I could add a notable fact about its later use by famous composers, which might interest the reader.</think>
Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart famously wrote a set of variations on this melody, but he did not compose the original tune.
